LAW AND MOTION 302, FRANCISCO GUTIERREZ'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD FOR PLAINTIFF MAHER MEMARZADEH: THE MOTION BY FRANCISCO GUTIERREZ TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL TO PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S LENGTHY DECLARATION CORROBORATES COUNSEL GUTIERREZ'S CONTENTION THAT THERE HAS BEEN A BREAKDOWN IN THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT RELATIONSHIP, AND ALSO MAKES CLEAR THAT COUNSEL AND PLAINTIFF DO NOT SEE EYE TO EYE WITH RESPECT TO THE STRATEGIC DIRECTION AND HANDLING OF THE CASE. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF ASSERTS IN THE DECLARATION THAT IT DOES NOT CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, THE COURT ON ITS OWN MOTION ORDERS THE DECLARATION SEALED TO PROTECT PRIVILEGED COMMUNICATIONS AND INFORMATION AND AVOID PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF. ORDER TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL AND ORDER TO SEAL ARE SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar Feb-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Maher Memarzadeh(310-310-5584; maher@ucla.edu), Plaintiff.
Nicholas J. Directo (ndirecto@selmanlaw.com) of Selman Breitman LLP for Defendant.
No appearance by moving party.

The Court notes Plaintiff did not comply with tentative ruling rule.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Francisco Gutierrez's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD for PLAINTIFF MAHER MEMARZADEH:

The motion by Francisco Gutierrez to be relieved as counsel to plaintiff is granted. Plaintiff's lengthy declaration corroborates counsel Gutierrez's contention that there has been a breakdown in the attorney-client relationship, and also makes clear that counsel and plaintiff do not see eye to eye with respect to the strategic direction and handling of the case. Although plaintiff asserts in the declaration that it does not contain confidential information, the court on its own motion orders the declaration sealed to protect privileged communications and information and avoid prejudice to plaintiff.

Order to be relieved as counsel and order to seal are signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT NAUTILUS INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is adopted, as follows:

Defendant Nautilus Insurance Company's demurrer to the bad faith cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff's allegations regarding Barry Vice's insufficient investigation of the claim are sufficient to support the claim. (Compl. 14, 18; see Shade Foods v. Innovative Products Sales & Marketing, Inc. (2000) 78 Cal.App.4th 847, 879-880 ["Among the most critical factors bearing on the insurer's good faith is the adequacy of its investigation of the claim. '[T]he covenant of good faith and fair dealing implied in all insurance agreements entails a duty to investigate properly submitted claims.' . . . . [] An unreasonable failure to investigate amounting to such unfair dealing may be found where an insurer fails to consider, or seek to discover, evidence relevant to the issues of liability and damages."].)
The demurrer to the fraud cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The general rule is that the elements of a fraud claim must be alleged with particularity. (See Committee on Childrens' Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 216.) In this case, plaintiff's allegations are conclusory and they fail "to show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered." (Hills Transp. Co. v. Southwest Forest Industries, Inc. (1968) 266 Cal.App.2d 702, 707.) The demurrer to the negligence cause of action is sustained without leave to amend as conceded.

Counsel for the plaintiff shall prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection,the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT NAUTILUS INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is adopted, as follows:

Defendant Nautilus Insurance Company's motion to strike punitive damage allegations from the first amended complaint is denied. In insurance cases, the same evidence is relevant both to a bad faith claim and the imposition of punitive damages, although the conduct required to award punitive damages must be of a "different dimension" than that required to find bad faith." (Shade Foods, 78 Cal.App.4th at 890.) The degree or "dimension" of Defendant's alleged misconduct in investigating the claim (if any), is a factual question that cannot be decided on a pleading motion.

Counsel for the plaintiff shall prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
